timebase.notes: Event timing is based on 1 fps, 2 fps, and 4 fps contact-sheet review. Use frame-exact still sequences before implementation tuning.

events.motion_hint: starfield with red title/mission presentation text
events.source_note: 4 fps subwindow review shows title/mission presentation becoming visible after the opening starfield/score header.

events.motion_hint: score advance table and convoy charger labels visible
events.source_note: Score-table subwindow covers 5.000s to 29.000s with 2 fps review cadence.

events.motion_hint: Namco text visible below score advance table
events.source_note: Namco presentation is visible near the end of the score-table sequence and before the rack handoff.

events.motion_hint: full rack visible with top-rank and lower-rank alien families
events.source_note: Rack becomes visible in the 34.000s to 42.000s handoff subwindow and remains visible through the late attract-pressure window.

events.motion_hint: game over text with player Galaxip visible near lower field
events.source_note: GAME OVER remains visible during the rack and late-pressure windows, so late action should be treated as attract/demo or post-game evidence until classified.

events.motion_hint: player Galaxip and a visible shot or threat marker appear near the end of the clip
events.source_note: Late-attract-pressure subwindow shows Galaxip, attacker/projectile or shot states, and explosions. Do not use as player-control authority yet because GAME OVER remains visible.

open_questions: Does the intro source include playable formation entry, attract/demo play, or only pre-game presentation?
Are enemy families readable enough for semantic tagging?
Is any usable audio present beyond the near-silent encoded track?
Should this become the preferred first beginning-phase source?
